headline: How Lowered 2026 Outlook And Cost Pressures At Clorox (CLX) Has Changed Its Investment Story
summary: In the past quarter, Clorox reported flat net sales and margin pressure from higher manufacturing and logistics costs, and management cut its fiscal 2026 outlook for both net sales and gross margin. These developments have sharpened investor focus on whether Clorox can offset persistent cost headwinds and restore profitability as it approaches its upcoming fourth-quarter results. Against this backdrop of lowered guidance and cost pressure, we'll now examine how these headwinds could...

headline: Has Clorox (CLX) Fallen Far Enough To Merit A Discount?
summary: Clorox stock has retreated sharply over the past few years, yet the current intrinsic value estimate from a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) view and the market based multiples both indicate the shares may still be priced below what the underlying cash flows suggest. Over the past 5 years, Clorox has declined 38.6%, which puts the current valuation in focus for investors willing to look past the share price weakness. Expectations around profit margins and cost pressures can support or undermine...

headline: Is Clorox (CLX) Undervalued Following Guidance Cuts And Upcoming Earnings?
summary: Clorox stock reacts to guidance cuts and upcoming earnings Clorox (CLX) has come under pressure as investors weigh its upcoming fourth quarter results against previously lowered full year guidance that pointed to ongoing sales declines and margin pressure from elevated costs. See our latest analysis for Clorox. At a share price of $96.56, Clorox has experienced short term share price pressure, with the 90 day share price return down 8.28% and the 1 year total shareholder return down 20.25%...
